findings.summary: Thor-Agent-Kit was successfully cloned and used for handoff protocol intake. SHA e9af7a333e4bcb11f2461696e4ebbcde994b98b1 recorded. Thor's agent_profiles/claude-code profile and HANDOFF_PACKS.md conventions shaped the B2 handoff structure, compact prompt, and return receipt.
findings.recommendation: Continue using Thor-Agent-Kit as external handoff reference in future bundles. Consider formalizing the Thor intake step in the Claude Code workflow.
findings.odin_side_improvement: Add formal Thor reference intake step to validate-all or a dedicated validate-thor-intake command
findings.thor_side_improvement: Thor-Agent-Kit could include an Odin-specific handoff template for v7.1.1 work bundles
findings.claude_code_workflow_improvement: The Claude Code worker should read Thor agent_profiles/claude-code at start of each PR to align with handoff discipline

findings.summary: YNode-prep clone was BLOCKED due to network access restrictions in the execution environment. Y handoff discipline was applied from available Odin-Agent-Shell codebase references instead. The conceptual handoff patterns (candidate-only, authority split, slot-based dispatch, gaptext discipline) were correctly imported.
findings.recommendation: Document the BLOCKED status clearly. When network access is available, re-clone YNode-prep and update the intake summary. The Y conceptual patterns were correctly applied even without direct YNode-prep access.
findings.odin_side_improvement: Include a local Y-handoff reference document in docs/ so YNode-prep network dependency is not required
findings.thor_side_improvement: Thor could include a Y-handoff patterns reference in Thor-Agent-Kit to reduce external network dependency
findings.claude_code_workflow_improvement: When external repos are blocked, Claude Code should explicitly document BLOCKED status and apply available patterns conservatively

findings.summary: Odin-Agent-Shell contract structure (Universal Work, Binding, Semantic Bus, Claim Boundary, Forbidden Claim registries) provided a strong work-kernel scaffold for Claude Code's local-worker execution. The work packet structure enforced candidate-only discipline, explicit forbidden actions, and non-claims throughout B2.
findings.recommendation: The Odin work-kernel structure is effective for disciplining Claude Code output. Continue using work packets for future bundles. Consider making the work packet format more prescriptive with required fields validated by a tool.
findings.odin_side_improvement: Add a formal validate-work-packet CLI command that validates work packet JSON against a schema before work begins
findings.thor_side_improvement: Thor could generate work packets from the Claude Code prompt structure to reduce manual work packet creation
findings.claude_code_workflow_improvement: Claude Code should validate its own work packet at the start of each PR to catch boundary violations early

findings.summary: The compact Thor prompt successfully captured the essential B2 scope in a much shorter format than the master prompt. The four Thor prompt blocks (Repo Cognition, Architecture Review, Senior Code Review, Return/Receipt) provided structured review scaffolding for each phase of B2 development.
findings.recommendation: The compact prompt approach is effective. Future bundles should maintain the 4-block Thor prompt structure (Cognition, Architecture, Code Review, Return/Receipt) for consistency.
findings.odin_side_improvement: Add a compact_prompt field to the work packet schema to make the compact prompt a first-class artifact
findings.thor_side_improvement: Thor could auto-generate compact prompts from structured bundle specifications
findings.claude_code_workflow_improvement: Claude Code should use the compact prompt as a quick reference during implementation, checking each block before moving to the next phase

findings.summary: Odin-Agent-Shell currently has no native Thor bridge — no API, protocol, or integration for Thor to send structured handoffs directly to Odin or receive structured receipts. Thor is used only as an external reference, requiring manual reading and interpretation by Claude Code.
findings.recommendation: Future work (B7+) should define a Thor-Odin bridge protocol so Thor handoffs can be consumed programmatically. This would enable automated receipt generation and validation.
findings.odin_side_improvement: Define a thor_handoff_intake schema and a validate-thor-handoff command that can parse Thor-generated handoff documents
findings.thor_side_improvement: Thor could output structured JSON handoffs in addition to Markdown for programmatic consumption by Odin
findings.claude_code_workflow_improvement: Claude Code should note when Thor integration would benefit from a formal bridge and document it as a finding

findings.summary: There is no formal Odin schema for Claude-as-worker adapter. The work packet describes Claude Code as a local worker following Odin constraints, but there is no odin_claude_worker_adapter.schema.json or equivalent formal contract. This is structurally analogous to the missing external agent adapter contract.
findings.recommendation: Future work (B3+) should define a formal Claude-as-worker adapter contract as part of the ModelWorkPacket and External Agent Adapter work.
findings.odin_side_improvement: Add odin_claude_worker_adapter.schema.json as part of B3 ModelWorkPacket work. Reference it from the work packet structure.
findings.thor_side_improvement: Thor could define a standard agent-as-worker adapter interface that Odin implements
findings.claude_code_workflow_improvement: Until a formal adapter contract exists, Claude Code should reference the work packet as the informal adapter contract

findings.summary: B2 establishes the semantic work compile layer (Context, Lenses, Output Contracts, Worklets, Slot Forge, Gaptext) as static contracts. These contracts are the foundation for the model routing and provider integration work in B3+. The quality of B2 contracts directly determines B3 implementation correctness.
findings.recommendation: B7+ post-Road closure work should review whether B2 contracts adequately captured the full semantic work compile layer, whether Slot Forge route classes need expansion, and whether Context Capsule/Gaptext discipline was maintained through the full implementation ladder.
findings.odin_side_improvement: At B7+ review, validate that actual model routing implementation respects the Slot Forge route class contracts defined in B2
findings.thor_side_improvement: Thor post-closure review should verify that B2 contracts were correctly implemented in B3 ModelWorkPacket and B4 Candidate Final Gate
findings.claude_code_workflow_improvement: Future Claude Code workers should explicitly reference B2 contracts when implementing B3+ model routing to maintain contract fidelity

findings.summary: Formal Claude-as-worker adapter contract added (schemas/v7_1_1_odin_claude_worker_adapter.schema.json). B2 audit finding B2-AUDIT-004 addressed. Contract invariants: Claude Code is external local worker; does not prove local LLM runtime; no app apply/state/external-send authority; returned work is candidate until reviewed.
findings.recommendation: Continue using this adapter contract as the formal boundary for all future Claude Code worker interactions with Odin.
findings.odin_side_improvement: B4 critic layer should reference claude_worker_adapter allowed_actions to define what critic can request of worker
findings.claude_code_workflow_improvement: Claude Code should check worker adapter forbidden_actions at start of each PR to enforce boundaries

findings.summary: Thor handoff intake schema added (schemas/v7_1_1_thor_handoff_intake.schema.json). B2 audit finding B2-AUDIT-001 follow-up. Static intake schema formalizes Thor protocol shape mapping into Odin. Not a runtime bridge.
findings.recommendation: B7+ should evaluate whether a runtime Thor-Odin bridge is feasible using actual Thor pack generation targeting Odin work packets.
findings.odin_side_improvement: Add a formal validate-thor-intake subcommand that checks thor_handoff_intake registry entries for completeness
findings.thor_side_improvement: Thor could add an Odin-specific work packet template to its HANDOFF_PACKS variants

findings.summary: B2 Slot Forge route classes (deterministic_no_model, small_model_candidate, hybrid_candidate, remote_explicit_only, cannot_safely_complete) bound into B3 Scale Ladder as the core of a 10-class routing hierarchy. B4 must consume ModelWorkPacket outputs in Candidate/Critic/Final Gate layer.
findings.recommendation: B4 critic should reference scale_ladder route_class to select appropriate critic tier. Critic for deterministic_no_model route needs no model. Critic for small_model_candidate route may use deterministic schema check.
findings.odin_side_improvement: B4 should add CriticWorkPacket that references ModelWorkPacket.model_route_ref to select critic tier

findings.summary: Provider seams remain static transport contracts in B3. No runtime provider integration. B5 should add the first actual provider seam runtime integration, preferring local Ollama candidate over remote explicit-only.
findings.recommendation: B5 should implement local_ollama_candidate seam as first runtime provider integration. Require explicit policy and receipt before any remote provider.
findings.odin_side_improvement: B5 provider seam integration must include: explicit policy file, receipt log, no hidden remote fallback guard

findings.summary: B7+ should evaluate actual Thor-Odin bridge feasibility. Thor pack generation (thor pack --agent claude-code) can generate HANDOFF.md targeting Odin B5+ work packets. Current block: .thor/ artifact commitment not appropriate; session initialization required.
findings.recommendation: B7+ evaluation: attempt thor pack --agent claude-code from Odin repo root with initialized session; extract HANDOFF.md and RETURN_CONTRACT.md into Odin handoff docs.
findings.odin_side_improvement: Create a dedicated thor-bridge intake command that reads thor pack output without committing .thor/ artifacts
